origin.name: Ethiopia
origin.key_document: Death certificate in Amharic (requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Ethiopia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Addis Ababa guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Ethiopia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate in Amharic (certified English translation required)
special_rules: Death is registered with the local Kebele (neighbourhood administration) promptly.
All Amharic-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Ethiopian authorities is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Ethiopian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Addis Ababa can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Ethiopia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Ethiopia. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-4 weeks. Kebele registration, Amharic translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
